review_text: I ordered a SACHS flywheel (2294 501 267) from Autodoc, paying a premium SACHS price.What I received was a broken, recycled LUK flywheel, inside a SACHS box, tied together with zip ties and taped shut. The flywheel had huge play throughout, making it completely unsafe for installation.SACHS/ZF confirmed that this flywheel is not even produced by SACHS, but sourced from LUK for rare VWAG models. Yet Autodoc never disclosed this anywhere, selling it as SACHS at a premium price.The price difference is shocking: in Bulgaria it can be €350–€450, while on other European sites it is only about €150, yet the product inside is identical to a much cheaper LUK part. Autodoc profits massively by misleading customers.This is misleading, unsafe, and completely unacceptable. I returned the part the same day, but Autodoc failed to resolve the issue, leaving my car unusable for 2 months and causing 4 months of delays, frustration, and extra costs.Autodoc must stop misleading customers and clearly disclose when parts are sourced from third parties, especially when they are broken or unsafe.
supplier_response: Hello Kristian Karakashev! We sincerely apologize for the experience you’ve had with the flywheel. We understand your frustration regarding the incorrect part, safety concerns, and the inconvenience caused by the delays. Please be informed that our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to update with your case in detail and assist you further to resolve this matter. Your feedback will also be used to improve our product verification and disclosure processes. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Very disappointing and misleading experience with AutodocMy experience with Autodoc has been extremely frustrating and disappointing.I ordered spare parts after Autodoc’s website explicitly confirmed compatibility with my vehicle. My VIN number is already saved in my personal account, and based on this information, the website assured that the parts were suitable. However, when the parts arrived, they turned out to be completely incompatible.As a result, I had to return the items and paid shipping costs twice (to and from Geneva), totaling approximately CHF 85. Autodoc refunded only the price of the parts and refused to reimburse the shipping costs, even though the mistake was entirely theirs.Instead of taking responsibility, they attempted to blame the customer by claiming that selecting parts by make and model is “not accurate.” This raises a serious question: why does their website confirm compatibility if it is unreliable?This was not my first bad experience with Autodoc. Previously, I was offered a compensation voucher for a similar mistake, which later turned out to be invalid and unusable. Now they repeat the same approach by offering a small discount coupon instead of refunding the actual costs caused by their error.In summary:Incorrect and incompatible partsCustomer forced to pay for the company’s mistakesUnreliable vouchersAvoidance instead of real solutionsA very poor customer experience. I will not use Autodoc again and cannot recommend them.
supplier_response: Hello Sabah Jassem! We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and frustration you’ve experienced. Please be aware that the parts displayed on our website whether selected through the vehicle selector or by entering your registration number are intended as general recommendations. Because vehicles often have unique specifications beyond just the model and body type, the most accurate way to confirm compatibility is by using the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) or the Original Equipment Number (OEN). Even if your VIN is stored in your account, our system does not automatically verify compatibility against it. This important step must be carried out by our trained specialists to ensure the correct fit. Our customer support team is always available to help with a full compatibility check, as product listings only provide technical details and cannot guarantee suitability for every vehicle. Please rest assured that our Quality Control Manager will be reaching out to you shortly to personally review your case and provide further assistance, ensuring the matter is handled properly. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered a steering rack for my 2009 Audi A4 B8 2.0l TDI (143hp) 2 weeks ago. The delivery was fast, it arrived in only a few days, which i liked. Yet it turns out a part was missing in the delivery: The metal plug. Instead, it was delivered with a plastic one to keep the dust from entering. According to my local mechanic, without the right plug / lock, the plastic one wouldn't keep up with the oil pressure and it would just explode.I have tried to contact AUTODOC in multiple ways.-The AI assistant on their app told me I'd get an e-mail in 24-48 hours, 4 days later I haven't received anything.-I have called their numbers in hope of reaching an actual human instead of an AI, but guess what. Dan the virtual assistant picked up and he was no help at all. I could repeat a sentence 3 times and he wouldn't understand a thing I said, so after some time he gave up and said "We're happy we could solve your problem!" Next I wanted to see if it's possible to atleast return the steering rack, but no, "Unfortunately, this product is not eligible for a refund."I spent a total of 480 bucks on this thing, and for someone at the age of 19, I simply cannot afford this waste of money.And even if this so called "plug" isn't actually included in the product, then at least help me find the missing piece I need.I might not be experienced in cars and I could also be wrong about what I said in the beginning, it might just be called something else or it might just be an entirely other piece that I'm talking about. My main point is that there is literally no way for me to reach out to AUTODOC because of their awfully designed AI assistant.
supplier_response: Hello ! We’re very sorry to read about your experience and understand how frustrating this situation must be, especially given the importance and cost of the part. 2 out of t: I ordered a set of headlights for my Polo in May after someone smashed them backing into me. I had them fitted with my annual service in October (I am self-employed and didn’t wanna lose an extra day of work and income). Upon fitting the garage realises one of the lights is broken so I issue a warranty claim. They send me a replacement part very quickly- so far, so good.However, the part I was sent is for brakes. So, I send it back using their return label. It arrives on the 24th of Nov (I have proof of tracking). And I wait. But no light. All I get is emails that details are missing. At first it’s my address. Then they say the returned parts haven’t been received. Last week their customer service assures me the case will be escalated and prioritised. Today they email me that they are closing it. More assurances that the warranty department will take care of it. Nobody can tell me what details are missing. There is no contact for the warranty department or an email address to contact them. I’m at a point where I just want a refund for the headlight I paid for.Summary: If nothing goes wrong they are a decent provider of parts. As soon as something goes wrong, it’s shambles and unnerving and either ineptitude or stalling tactics. At the moment, would absolutely not recommend.Update 19/12/2025: Going up from 2 Stars to 3.5After posting my initial review they contacted me and settled he issue swiftly. All resolved but it's still not impressive that it needs a Trustpilot review to get proper help. Will use them again but cautiously. They have to win back full trust. Once that's achieved I'll happily review again.

review_text: After posting this complaint, I was eventually contacted and reimbursed for the incompatible parts. However, after a few messages, the employee who had initially reached out to me appeared to stop responding, which forced me to contact the in-app support again. Only then was a warranty claim filed and my money refunded. While I am grateful for the resolution, I remain concerned about the way it was handled.The company referred to this outcome as a ‘gesture of goodwill.’ In reality, complying with consumer protection law is not goodwill—it is a legal obligation. What troubles me most is that, instead of straightforward assistance, I personally had to spend a great deal of time researching laws, studying specific legal articles, and presenting them to prove that I had the right to a refund.I appreciate the final resolution, but the process was unnecessarily time-consuming, frustrating, and placed the entire burden on me as the customer.Order number: 119912765Order date: 10.09.2024I am extremely disappointed with Autodoc’s customer service and their refusal to take responsibility for an incorrect part selection.All my previous orders for my Honda Accord 8 (VIN: JHMCU35809C203590) were always placed through Autodoc’s support team, never by myself. I always relied on their staff to provide the correct parts. However, in this case Autodoc now falsely claims that I ordered the parts on my own without their involvement, which is absolutely not true.The lower control arms that I received do not fit my vehicle. This is a clear case of “Sachmangel” under §434 BGB (German Civil Code) — the goods do not conform to the contract, as they are not suitable for the intended purpose. According to §439 BGB, in such cases the seller is obliged to provide a correct replacement or a full refund, and also to compensate the customer for costs of removal/installation.Instead, Autodoc is trying to hide behind a 14-day return period, which is irrelevant here. The 14-day return policy only applies to voluntary returns. When the goods are not as described or do not match the contract due to the seller’s mistake, the time limit does not apply — this is confirmed by EU consumer law (Directive 1999/44/EC and Directive (EU) 2019/771 on consumer sales and guarantees).In other words:	•	The parts were selected incorrectly by Autodoc staff (as in all my previous orders).	•	The parts do not fit — this is legally a non-conforming good.	•	Autodoc must provide a replacement or refund under BGB §§434–439 and EU Directive 2019/771.	•	Refusing support and shifting blame onto the customer is a breach of EU consumer rights.I am now forced to escalate this case to the European Consumer Centre (ECC-Net) and file a formal complaint. This whole experience has shown me that Autodoc’s customer support is unwilling to take responsibility when their own mistake causes problems for the buyer.⚠️ Buyers beware: if Autodoc makes a mistake in selecting your parts, they will try to deny responsibility and hide behind irrelevant “return policies.”
supplier_response: Hello Александр Пятковский! We regret you faced all the inconvenience. Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Ordered parts for my vehicle - Parts arrived and were incorrect; OK not an issue happens, but it is how this is dealt with that separates a good company and bad.  Followed their system for returns, can't talk on chat to a real person only a "bot" and AI really is not there yet!  Got the return authorised and and a return label was sent.  First I had to return a very heavy parcel through the post office and because the return was to Berlin special documentation for customs.  The return was delivered quickly on 5th July - I did not get my refund until 18th July, and when it did it was short by GBP4 - went online to autodoc site to delete account and took 30minutes just to get to live chat person - who then just didn't respond  - as usually they are dealing with more than 1 chat room query, but to be sat there for over 5 minutes without a response is just annoying to say the least.  As always, if the order has no issues I am sure they are a fine supplier, but as always a supplier should be judge on their handling of an issue and Autodoc in my opinion is at the bottom of this list.  If Autodoc are reading this my order no is 116362455 - knock yourselves out, as you will see what I have said above is correct!
supplier_response: Hello RJ, We are sorry that didn't meet your expectations. Please check the order number that you have provided. This order doesn't belong to you. Please contact us at trust@autodoc.co.uk and provide correct order details and our quality control team will check your case. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered some parts from AUTODOC and when they arrived in the UK at the EVRI parcel delivery depo, the EVRI tracking system told me “This parcel is bigger than we expected, so a specialist Evri courier will need to deliver it.”   So it is coming up to 5 days that the parcel has been with EVRI, and I am still waiting to hear when my parcel is going to be delivered.   I contacted AUTODOC told them the parcel was oversized.   AUTODOC said “It is very unlikely that the package was oversized. All parts are inside normal package 750x200x950”.  I have confirmed with EVRI and their size limit is “Add together the 2 shortest sides and multiply it by 2. Then add the length. If the total is less than 245cm, your good to go”.   So doing this with the dimensions AUTODOC gave me for my parcel (750x200x950) the calculation would be 200 +  750 = 950.   Then 950 x 2 = 1900.  Then take the 1900 + 950 = 2850!   A total of 2850mm shows this parcel is over the EVRI size limit of 2450mm.   So it is oversized for EVRI based on AUTODOC’s own admissions.   EVRI also tells parcel senders “If your parcel is Overweight or Oversized then we’ll send the additional costs on to the sender and keep the parcel until we receive payment”.   So if AUTODOC don’t admit what they have done and look into what I have told them (will AUTODOC do this, because as far as AUTODOC are concerned the delivery is still not classified as late or there isn’t a size issue as far as they are concerned) so looking at other complaints on Trustpilot this could be weeks before this is sorted out.I have to also add this is the Second order in a row now, I have had a delivery issue with.  So I have 4 direct questions for AUTODOC which are as follows.  1)	Why do you send out oversized parcels to EVRI?   2)	Even if this was an AUTODOC accident sending it via EVRI, why does you customer service team not know about the size limit with EVRI and go immediately to resolve the problem?  3)	Is the issue I have having also happening to others?    4)	When am I going to get my parcel?   AUTODOC if you reply to this post, please give actual direct answers to all my questions and not some standard holding comment like “Your case has been referred to the quality control department and will be considered as soon as possible.”  Thank you.  John
supplier_response: Hello John, We are sorry you faced inconveniences. We will check situation with the parcels' sizes. Our quality control manager will contact you in the nearest time. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

Few months ago I purchased a product on eBay from ATP Autotelie, now it broke because of a clear defect (it broke in two parts), and due to its breakage I was close to get my car engine completely burned. I had to spend more money to replace the defective product on an emergency basis, with a lot of time waste.Not even three months were passed, so the product was and still is under purchase warranty coverage, which is defined by the UE Council directive 85/374/CEE of 25th July 1985.However, ATP Autotelie has blocked all ways to contact them or rise any kind of issue.Therefore, I tried try to assert my customer rights through the PayPal claiming system, but the seller invented the excuse that somehow I “altered” the product, which is clearly false and can be easily verified from the picture I sent them. Anyway, PayPal decided to support the fraud and rejected my claim.The matter does not end here, I am reporting what happened (all of which can be proven with documentation in hand) first here on TrustPilot, and then I am turning to a consumer defense association to take legal action, not only against the seller but also against PayPal.This is not so much for an economic problem, but because people cannot be defrauded in this way, it is a question of principle and a question of law, both national and European community laws.**** Update ****After the review was published on TrustPilot, I was contacted by ATP Autoteile, unlocking the possibility to message them on eBay messaging system (but why it was blocked, nobody said).They behaved like they didn't know anything about the claim, that looks quite suspicious to me: did eBay arbitrarily take the initiative to reject my complaint without even informing the seller and without any reasoning? Let's take this as the truth, since the level of eBay service is so poor that can actually arrive at this point.After this, they formally apologized (formally) and accepted to refund the faulty product (except shipping costs), without wanting the broken product back. Then. we had days and days of endless email exchange to undergo the steps of the refund process, several their emails were also sent me twice from different email address. If I could count all the time I lost to reply and send all the information they asked, probably I had to receive three-four times the due amount, but probably this was exactly the idea under such behavior.Obviously I just received the refund of the faulty product (except shipping costs), that is already a huge step on towards the right direction, but the experience was so frustrating that I cannot avoid helping others to avoid my same mistake.

review_text: Your typical shady online retailer. Good prices, fast delivery as long as there's no problem, ordered quite a few things but never more. That's when they show their true morale. When they see it's a never ending story (completely their own fault), they decided it'll probably cost to much to honor the agreement (despite me spending hundreds of euro for a lot of parts in the same order)  and just back away leaving me stranded witouth the most cruicial part, one out of four shock absorbers. The "customer service" is a joke, they have no feeling for right and wrong and will never go outside the manuscript, regardless if they messed up completely and put a customer in a seriously bad situation. Even the people in charge of customer service totaly lacks basic knowledge or just don't care.Will be posting the email conversations when I have the time so everyone can read it and weep. More than 70 emails back and forth over the course of almost 3 months to deal with the whole shitstorm with a bad ending (yeah, that's right) so will pack it up nicely.Stay away, trust me. Truly, truly horrible organisation.EDITReply to ATP:s reply: Yes, you refunded me the for the "second" shipping cost because you should have added that item in the forst order, you lost my order, it was greatly delayedand you sent me the wrong item in an opened and beat up box. So I have paid ONE shipping cost already according to our agreement, but still not recieved the item you agreed to send free of shipping.When you sent me the wrong shockabsorber in a opened and beat up box, which I sent back to you, you only refunded the shockabsorber and you refuse to send me a new one without me paying for the shipping cost again!Maybe it's easier to understand if I put it like this:1. First order. Lost, delayed and wrongly handled. Shipping paid.2. Second order. The item that should have been added to the first one (plus I added a bunch of other things=more money for you). Shipping cost paid again, and for the item of course.3. ONE shipping cost refunded according to agreement. (1 of 2 shipping costs paid)4. Wrong item recieved and returned.5. Wrong item refunded only. (Back at square one, with me missing an item that should have been sent in the first order.)6. You refuse to send a new one for some absurd reason, even though you have sent me the wrong one. Only reply is to place a new order and pay for shipping AGAIN.Do you still not understand?!

review_text: I’m pretty sure that this review makes no difference to ATP or my case, but hopefully people will think twice before buying anything from this company.I purchased a Sachs clutch kit from ATP, for my Audi A4. The payment and delivery went fine and quick, which is probably the only positive thing I have to say about this company.We had scheduled a repair at a local mechanic for a gearbox and clutch replacement, so we delivered the ATP clutch as per agreement to the mechanic. However, when the mechanic went to fit the new clutch to the car, he noticed that the clutch didn’t fit and was actually a LUK clutch (completely different brand to Sachs). The clutch was branded LUK on one side and Sachs on the other. As the car was taking up space on a lift and the mechanic couldn’t wait for a new clutch to be delivered, we had to buy the correct clutch locally. Unfortunately it didn’t come with a clutch release bearing, so we had to use the one supplied with the clutch kit from ATP.I called ATP and explained the situation (in German, as apparently only one or two ATP employees speaks English) and asked if it was possible for me to return the incorrect clutch without the bearing, to which they replied yes. I returned the clutch as agreed, and for a long period of time I heard nothing from them, so I decided to call them to see if they received the clutch. After a couple of calls, they informed me that they had in fact received the clutch, but that the bearing was missing and that they could not refund me as the set was not complete. I tried to explain the situation several times with no luck and no sympathy.ATP sells the exact clutch release bearing on their website and I informed them, that I would happily pay for the bearing that I had used from the kit, so they could replace it in the kit. But for some reason this is impossible to do for ATP. In theory I could just get the clutch back, buy the bearing, put it in the box and return a complete set. I have now spent several hours on the phone, trying to first of all get through to them on the phone, which is a nightmare. And when I finally manage to get through, nobody speaks English (fortunately I speak a little German). The employees that answer the phone cannot make any decisions and needs to speak to their supervisors and promises to call back, which they NEVER do, so you have to start all over every time you get through to them.To make things even better, now ATP wants me to pay them to return the wrong clutch to me.

(They were included in the price and shown on the website.)  Because the bad quality it could not be fitted with the original Renault or other clamps. The flange was much to low. After a every email they promised me to send the missing parts within a few days. But they didn't. Tried to call them but could not reach them. (Tried it 17 times one afternoon, they put you on hold and then disconnect after 5-10 minutes waiting.)So brought the car to garage where they welded the catalytic converter to the exhaust system. To my surprise, after 10 weeks or more, I got without any warning the missing parts in the mail! Thanks!Real sad company, they don't even try to help their customers!

review_text: "no risk 90 day money back guarantee". Yeah sure.Bought a Chinese made A/C compressor for my Mercedes from them. It leaked badly. Not because it was broken or something. The design was bad and impossible to  create a sealed system.Sent the unit back. They made every single excuse you could imagine - I hadn't given them a chance to fix the problem, so no warranty (I did  they answered no questions about making the product actually work). - I didn't include the original mechanic's invoice - (I did, right on top of the compressor)- They inspected the thing and claimed  there was no proof of the defect occuring before the installation (simply, the replacement part was of a bad design - didn't work before, didn't work after, despite trying many things to prevent having to pay to uninstall and then install a different one)- The invoice didn't show what they needed to see - no warranty (my Italian speaking mechanic hardly understands what I ask him in english, however, I made sure he did the job right - flushing, replacing the drying unit, etc.)In short, they kept the part (probably re-sold it) and kept the money also!Edited October 25, 2018: ATP tried hard to remove this review about a year ago. Fortunately, after trustpilot looked at the evidence, this review remained. Don't be fooled by the feeble bluff of an offer to resolve the issue more than two years after the fact. Do yourself a favour and listen to my and all others' experience in buying cheap, poorly made non-functional parts and purchase your car parts from a reputable source. Otherwise, we told you so. My mistake cost me 179 euros for the compressor plus nearly 300 euros extra for my mechanic's huge efforts to try to make the thing work and then remove it and replace it with a unit from Mercedes.
